name: Herb Nut Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1 Tbsp minced fresh chives, or green onion tops
1/2 cup Chopped pine nuts or almonds
1 Tbsp Minced fresh parsley OR 1 Tbsp Dried parsley
1 cup Water heated to 120 to 130 degrees
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine filling ingredients; mix well and refrigerate.
2. Grease a 13 x 9-inch pan.
3. In a large bowl, combine flour mixture with yeast from foil packet, pine nuts, parsley, basil, tarragon, and garlic powder; mix well.
4. Stir in hot water, butter, and egg until the dough pulls away from the sides of the bowl.
5. Turn the dough out onto lightly floured surface. With greased or floured hands, shape the dough into a ball.
6. Knead the dough 5 minutes until smooth, sprinkling with additional flour if necessary to reduce stickiness.
7. Cover the dough with a large bowl; let rest for 5 minutes.
8. Divide the cream cheese mixture into 12 equal pieces; flatten each to a 3-inch circle.
9. Place a cheese piece on the center of each circle.
10. Wrap the dough around the cheese, pressing the edges of the dough to seal.
11. Place rolls, seam side down, in the greased pan.
12. Cover loosely with greased plastic wrap and cloth towel.
13. Let rise in a warm place for 30 minutes or until doubled in size.
14. Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
15. Uncover the dough. In a small bowl, beat egg white and milk until well blended; brush over rolls.
16. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
17. Bake for 20 to 30 minutes or until golden brown. Serve warm.
